events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to diagnose a fault from the evidence. The scenario: VFDs & motor controls observed during operation after a reported concern was checked. Readings are steady, no abnormal sound or heat, maintenance log shows standard functional check completed with no open punch note. Reference material: NEC 430, mfr manuals. So there is no fault found. The system appears to be operating normally. The reported concern was checked and no issue found. So decision should be "pass" (no fault). Risk: low, since no abnormal conditions. Work stage: in-service (as given). Component conditions: none flagged, so empty list. Percent complete: 100%? The work is in-service, so it's complete. But the task says "percent_complete is your numeric estimate of overall progress." Since it's in-service, it's fully complete. So 100. Value: no numeric reading asked, so 0. Sound source: not asked, so empty string? The schema says "component or source of the sound, when asked" - not asked, so maybe empty string. Confidence: high, say 0.95. Event time: 0. Rate: 0. Order: empty list. Workable: empty list. Findings: no defects, so empty list. Actions: no corrective actions needed, so empty list. Rationale: "No fault found; readings steady, no abnormal sound/heat, maintenance log clear." References: NEC 430, mfr manuals. So I'll output JSON accordingly.
